headline: Chevron's Board of Directors approved 2026 executive compensation packages, including base salary increases and equity awards for the CEO and other Named Executive Officers.
investor_impact: This is a routine annual compensation update that aligns executive pay with standard corporate governance practices. The heavy weighting of equity awards toward performance shares and RSUs ensures management remains incentivized to drive long-term shareholder value, presenting no material change to the broader investment thesis.
key_figures: Michael K. Wirth (CEO) base salary increased by $75,000 to $1,975,000
Michael K. Wirth equity grant award value of $23,000,000
Eimear P. Bonner (CFO) base salary increased by $50,000 to $1,100,000
Eimear P. Bonner equity grant award value of $5,094,000
Mark A. Nelson (Vice Chairman) base salary increased by $25,000 to $1,350,000
Mark A. Nelson equity grant award value of $8,533,800
R. Hewitt Pate (Chief Legal Officer) base salary increased by $50,000 to $1,250,000
R. Hewitt Pate equity grant award value of $7,641,000
Equity awards comprise 50% performance shares and 50% restricted stock units
Target bonus percentages under the Chevron Incentive Plan remain unchanged (Wirth 165%, Bonner 110%, Nelson 120%, Pate 110%)
